choicesEnglish: I would like to rent two bicycles.

correctAnswerEnglish: I would like to rent two bicycles.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Questa è la frase corretta per iniziare il noleggio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! This is the correct phrase to start the rental.

choicesItalian: Quanto costa per tutto il giorno?
choicesEnglish: How much does it cost for the whole day?
How much does it cost for one hour?
How much does it cost for a week?
How much does it cost for a month?
correctAnswerItalian: Quanto costa per tutto il giorno?
correctAnswerEnglish: How much does it cost for the whole day?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Chiedere il prezzo giornaliero è molto comune.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Asking for the daily price is very common.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. Ask for the entire day.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! È importante sapere cosa è compreso nel prezzo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! It's important to know what is included in the price.

choicesItalian: Ho un problema con la catena.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Segnalare un guasto è fondamentale per la sicurezza.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Reporting a fault is fundamental for safety.

choicesEnglish: Can you exchange it for another one?
Can you repair it right away?

correctAnswerEnglish: Can you exchange it for another one?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Se la bici è guasta, puoi chiederne una sostitutiva.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! If the bike is broken, you can ask for a replacement.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Cercare percorsi sicuri è un'ottima idea.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, piste ciclabili significa bike lanes.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Looking for safe routes is a great idea.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, bike lanes is piste ciclabili.

feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Getting local advice helps discover beautiful places.

choicesItalian: A che ora aprite la mattina?

feedback.correctEnglish: Right! It's important to respect the return time.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! La sicurezza prima di tutto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Safety first.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Sapere la distanza aiuta a pianificare la giornata.

feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Asking about the difficulty is prudent.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. Ask if it is difficult.

feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A new bike is usually more reliable.
